sections.paragraph:
m.-

(1) a curb or halter, which goes over a horse’s nose, Isa 30:28 gener. a bridle, Psa 32:9. Job 30:11, רֶסֶן מִפָּנַי שִׁלֵּחוּ “they cast off the bridle before me,” i.e. they use unbridled license; compare the Arabic phrase طلق زمامه to loose his halter, of an unbridled person. Hence-

(2) the inner part of the mouth, where the bit (das Gebiß) is put, like the Greek χαλινοί, teeth, Job 41:5, כֶּפֶל רִסְנֹו used of the double row of teeth (of the crocodile).

(3) [Resen], pr. name of a very ancient city in Assyria, Gen 10:12.
